hook.line1: AI is already in daily decisions.
hook.line2: The real question is how far we delegate judgment.
hook.question: BufferLine makes that boundary visible and operable.

everydayShift.title: Judgment shifts start quietly.
everydayShift.subtitle: Not with dramatic failures, but with everyday habits.

1.text: People now read AI answers first when comparing health and finance options.

2.text: Founders and teams begin with AI for hypotheses, priorities, and first drafts.

3.text: Final decisions remain, but the moment of judgment transfer is often lost.

everydayShift.talkTrack: Convenience grows quickly. Visibility into delegation usually does not.

awareness.title: Run three quick checks.
awareness.subtitle: If these are hard to answer, judgment flow is already drifting.

1.title: Do you feel the speed?
1.description: AI is replacing real work units faster than most teams notice.

2.title: Do you see the influence?
2.description: AI changes not only outputs, but how decisions are framed.

3.title: Do you remember the start point?
3.description: In key decisions, did you start from your own reasoning or from AI direction?

awareness.talkTrack: Surprise points differ by person, but the pattern is the same: AI is now inside judgment flow.

coreGap.title: Awareness reveals structural gaps.
coreGap.subtitle: This is not a model-quality issue. It is an operating issue.

observation.title: Observation Gap
observation.description: We cannot clearly track when judgment moved.

policy.title: Policy Gap
policy.description: Personal reasoning rules remain implicit and disappear.

authority.title: Authority Gap
authority.description: Decision rights lack explicit, testable criteria.

coreGap.talkTrack: Without closing these gaps, productivity rises while judgment sovereignty weakens.

cognitiveOs.title: The answer is not one feature. It is a 3-layer operating model.
cognitiveOs.subtitle: Each layer stands alone. Together they become a system.
cognitiveOs.talkTrack: Connected together, they move AI usage from convenience to accountable operation.

jdvp.title: JDVP turns judgment transfer into data.
jdvp.subtitle: Use logs, not memory, to understand delegation.

principle.title: Principle
principle.description: Do not score delegation as good or bad first. Make it visible first.

items.1: Turns intuition-heavy delegation debates into evidence-based discussion.
items.2: The same logs can support audits, retrospectives, and model operations.

items.1: Metrics without context can misrepresent human intent.
items.2: Delegation telemetry should not be misused as simplistic performance ranking.

jdvp.talkTrack: The key win is removing the state of 'I do not know when I delegated.'

thinkprint.title: Thinkprint makes reasoning style reusable.
thinkprint.subtitle: Convert tacit judgment into editable, shareable policy.

good.title: Strengths
good.body: Teams keep the logic behind decisions, not just the final answer.

caution.title: Watch-outs
caution.body: If treated as a fixed personality label, it narrows thinking. Keep it editable and versioned.

thinkprint.talkTrack: Thinkprint is not about labeling people. It is about making reasoning collaborative.

metagov.title: Meta Governance designs how decision rights move.
metagov.subtitle: The question is not only who decides, but who is authorized to decide under which conditions.

signal.title: Qualification Signals
signal.description: Define participation conditions from evidence, context, and role requirements.

renewal.title: Authority Renewal
renewal.description: Refresh decision rights as risk, role, and outcomes change.

defense.title: Abuse Defense
defense.description: Prevent concentration of authority, blame shifting, and opaque automation.

metagov.caution: Without authority design, even strong models collapse into accountability gaps.
metagov.talkTrack: BufferLine is not a model recommendation tool. It is an operating system for judgment responsibility.

close.title: BufferLine makes judgment sovereignty operational.
close.subtitle: It links observation, reasoning policy, and authority design into one system.

points.3: Design decision-right architecture with Meta Governance.
